cf_parsed.subtask_body:
I did one more global geometric reconstruction instead of tiny swaps.
A much cleaner perimeter/cluster tour is:
13-0-19-18-15-14-10-12-7-3-11-16-8-9-2-4-5-17-6-1-13
Rationale: west/north chain 1-6-17-5-4-2-9 is natural; east side 12-7-3 and 10-11 are short; center 15-14-10 is tighter than sending 15 to 12 directly.
I compared this against the incumbent by rough edge totals:
old had costly edges 0-1, 1-6, 12-15, and closure 14-13.
new has costly 13-0 and 1-13, but replaces several medium-long links with shorter local ones, especially 18-0-1... becoming 19-18-15-14 and 14-10-12.
I also considered variants with ...14-16-11-10-12... and ...3-7-12...; these seemed slightly worse than using 3-11 and 10-12.
Rough manual sum for the new tour is about 419, clearly better than the previous incumbent around low 430s.
No obvious crossings remain in the new candidate.
Best tour from this subtask:
13,0,19,18,15,14,10,12,7,3,11,16,8,9,2,4,5,17,6,1
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: New incumbent is the global reconstructed tour. If continuing, focus only on whether 10/11/12/3/7 ordering or the southwest start/end around 13/0/19/18/15 can be improved by one final 2-opt-style adjustment.

parsed.subtask_body:
I checked the most promising local repairs by rough edge-length comparison.
The prior tour has a very expensive edge 0-1 (~35) and also 1-6 (~29), suggesting city 1 should sit near the north-west chain, not between 0 and 6.
A better west/north order is 13-19-18-0-6-17-5-4-2-1..., because 2-1 (~49) is large but 1 closes naturally toward the center/east better than 0-1-6.
I then compared where to attach the center/east block. Putting 1 directly before 14 is attractive since 1-14 (~38) and 14-13 closure disappears if 13 starts the cycle.
I also checked replacing east chain ...16-11-10-3-7-12-15-14 with ...16-14-15-12-10-11-3-7... and variants; these tended to worsen because 11-3 and 7-12 are already quite natural, while 15 is much closer to 14/18/19 than to the far east.
Most useful improvement found is to move 1 from near 0 to between 2 and 14, yielding:
13-19-18-0-6-17-5-4-2-1-9-8-16-11-10-3-7-12-15-14
But 1-9 is terrible, so reject.
Better:
13-19-18-0-6-17-5-4-2-9-8-16-11-10-3-7-12-15-14-1
This replaces 0-1 and 1-6 by 0-6 and 14-1, but forces 1-13 closure; net seems slightly worse than current.
Another candidate:
13-19-18-0-6-17-5-4-2-1-14-15-12-7-3-10-11-16-8-9
This has plausible local adjacency but likely suffers from long 2-1 and 9-13 closure.
The strongest simple candidate I found is:
13-19-18-0-6-17-5-4-2-9-8-16-14-15-12-10-11-3-7-1
with closure 1-13; however 7-1 is huge, so reject.
After these checks, the original incumbent still looks best among tested tours: good regional monotonicity, short east chain 10-3-7-12, and decent center chain 8-16-11.
I did not find a clearly superior valid local reordering.
parsed.updated_plan_state: Current tour remains the incumbent. One more subtask could target a more global reconstruction: place city 1 near the top-left while simultaneously reconnecting the center block {14,15,16} and east block {10,11,12,3,7}, since isolated local swaps were inconclusive.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Start from geometric structure, not the weak baseline. Build an outer-hull style cycle, then insert interior cities in locally cheapest positions.
Refine by checking obvious crossing removals and 2-opt style edge swaps mentally.
Use city clusters: southwest {13,19,0,18}, west/northwest {6,17,5,4,2}, center {15,14,16,8,9}, east {12,10,11,3,7}.
Aim for a monotone loop around the outside: south/west up to north, across to east, then down and back southwest.
Then place interior cities 14,15,16,10,11,12 where they connect naturally and avoid long diagonals.
Compare a few candidate orders near the north cluster {2,4,5,6,17} and east cluster {3,7,10,11,12}.
Final subtask should output best feasible tour found and estimated length relative to baseline.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct a strong feasible tour from geometric ordering and improve it with mental crossing removal/local swaps", "p_solve": 0.72, "time_budget_s": 240}

SUB_1: Built a perimeter-first tour and checked for obvious crossings.
A strong candidate is to sweep southwest→west→north→east→south:
13-0-18-19-15-14-17-6-5-4-2-9-8-16-11-10-3-7-12-1-13 looked poor because 1 is misplaced.
A better structure is:
13-19-18-0-6-17-5-4-2-9-8-16-11-10-3-7-12-15-14-1-13, but 1-13 is long and east side can be improved.
Using 1 near the northwest chain and 15/14 in the center gives a cleaner loop:
13-19-18-0-1-2-4-5-17-6-9-8-16-11-10-3-7-12-15-14-13.
This has no obvious geometric crossings except maybe around 0-1-2 versus 6 chain; still likely much better than baseline.
I also compared placing 12 between 10 and 3 versus after 7; between 7 and 19 is clearly bad, between 10 and 3 is bad, so keep 7-12-15 transition or 10-12-7.
A more natural east descent is 3-7-12, since 3-7 is very short and 7-12 moderate.
Center chain 15-14-16-11-10 seems plausible, but 12 is closer to 10/3 than to 15.
Best refined candidate found:
13-19-18-0-1-6-17-5-4-2-9-8-16-11-10-3-7-12-15-14
Closure 14-13 is acceptable; 0-1 and 1-6 are both moderate.
This respects regional adjacency better than baseline, which had several long jumps like 6-15 and 12-3/3-7 isolated.
Estimated length roughly low 430s, perhaps high 420s if lucky.
BEST_GUESS: {"tour":[13,19,18,0,1,6,17,5,4,2,9,8,16,11,10,3,7,12,15,14]}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Keep the current valid geometric tour. Spend one more subtask testing a few 2-opt style reorderings around the west/north chain (0,1,6,17,5,4,2) and the center-east descent (16,11,10,12,3,7,15,14), aiming to remove any hidden crossings and shorten closure.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_2":0.04,"p_gap_le_5":0.18,"p_gap_le_10":0.58}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.42,"expected_delta_score":2.6,"expected_gap_reduction":3.5}
DECISION: continue
NEXT_SUB: {"id":2,"desc":"Try a handful of mental 2-opt/local reorderings on west/north and center-east segments; keep the best valid tour","p_solve":0.42,"time_budget_s":180}
